description: Accessing a DECLARED struct field does not fire E-TYPE-004 (§14.3). `p.x` names a field the `Point` type declares, so the nominal field-access check stays silent. The clean twin of e-type-004-struct-field-access-pos.
rationale: §14.3 a member access naming a DECLARED field is well-typed; E-TYPE-004 fires only on an undeclared field.
